_meta.author: Charles Haddon Spurgeon (1834-1892)
_meta.notes: Curated public-domain quotations attributed to C.H. Spurgeon. Sources noted broadly (sermon collection, devotional, or treatise). Verify exact citations against the original works as needed.

items.text: A Bible that's falling apart usually belongs to someone who isn't.
Begin the day with God; kneel down to Him in prayer; lift up thy heart to His abode, and seek His love to share.
Defend the Bible? I would as soon defend a lion! Unchain it and it will defend itself.
Visit many good books, but live in the Bible.
Nobody ever outgrows Scripture; the book widens and deepens with our years.
I have a great need for Christ; I have a great Christ for my need.
If Christ is not all to you, He is nothing to you. He will never go into partnership as a part Saviour of men.
He who does not believe that God forgives all sin, except the sin of unbelief, has touched the foul spot.
It is not great faith, but true faith, that saves; and the salvation lies not in the faith, but in the Christ in whom faith trusts.
Faith goes up the stairs that love has built and looks out the windows which hope has opened.
I have learned to kiss the wave that throws me against the Rock of Ages.
When you cannot trace His hand, trust His heart.
God is too good to be unkind, and He is too wise to be mistaken; and when we cannot trace His hand, we must trust His heart.
Anxiety does not empty tomorrow of its sorrows, but only empties today of its strength.
Our anxiety does not empty tomorrow of its sorrow; it only empties today of its joy.
Discernment is not knowing the difference between right and wrong. It is knowing the difference between right and almost right.
Wisdom is the right use of knowledge. To know is not to be wise. To know how to use knowledge is to have wisdom.
Humility is to make a right estimate of one's self.
Be content with such things as you have, for the Lord Himself has said, 'I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.'
If sinners be damned, at least let them leap to Hell over our dead bodies. And if they perish, let them perish with our arms wrapped about their knees, imploring them to stay.
Have you no wish for others to be saved? Then you are not saved yourself; be sure of that.
Every Christian is either a missionary or an impostor.
If you wish to know God, you must know His Word. If you wish to perceive His power, you must see how He works by His Word.
The gospel is preached in the ears of all men; it only comes with power to some.
He who counts the stars and calls them by their names, is in no danger of forgetting His own children.
There are no crown-wearers in heaven who were not cross-bearers here below.
I bear my willing witness that I owe more to the fire, and the hammer, and the file, than to anything else in my Lord's workshop.
Trials teach us what we are; they dig up the soil, and let us see what we are made of.
The Lord gets His best soldiers out of the highlands of affliction.
Prayer is the slender nerve that moves the muscles of omnipotence.

It is not the arithmetic of our prayers, how many they are; nor the rhetoric of our prayers, how eloquent they may be; nor the geometry of our prayers, how long they may be; nor the music of our prayers, how sweet our voice may be; nor the logic of our prayers, how argumentative they may be; nor the method of our prayers, how orderly they may be — which God cares for.
True prayer is measured by weight, not by length. A single groan before God may have more fullness of prayer in it than a fine oration of great length.
Whenever you do find yourself sleepless, think of God, and ten to one some sweet meditation will lull you to sleep.
Carve every word before you let it fall.
A good character is the best tombstone. Those who loved you and were helped by you will remember you when forget-me-nots have withered.
Sin is the mother and nurse of all evil, the egg of all mischief, the fountain of bitterness, the root of misery.
Of two evils, choose neither.
We are all at times unconscious prophets.
If God should cast out of His mouth every Christian who is lukewarm, where should we all be?
If you are not seeking the Lord, the Devil is seeking you. If you are not at work for the Lord, the Devil is at work for you.
The Holy Spirit will not dwell where there is strife. Let us settle all matters that are between us as Christian men.
Wherever the gospel comes, it claims absolute submission.
I would rather be the means of saving a soul from death than be the greatest orator on earth.
He who would write on people's hearts must labor much in private.
Brethren, do something; do something; do something! While societies and unions make constitutions, let us win souls.
I would rather be the means of saving one soul from going down to hell than be the greatest preacher who ever lived.
Soul winning is the chief business of the Christian minister; indeed, it should be the main pursuit of every true believer.
A sermon without Christ is an abomination.
Take a text and make a beeline to the cross.
There is no chamber of our nature that we can lock up and say to Christ, 'You shall not enter.'
Grace is given not because we have done good works, but in order that we may be able to do them.
When prayer leads the van, deliverance brings up the rear.
The Lord's mercy often rides to the door of our heart upon the black horse of affliction.
Sometimes God puts His children to bed in the dark.
We have not so much to face the storms of life as to outride them; faith is our anchor.
He who has God for his portion has all things in one.
Christ has nothing in His service like 'easy places.' He calls us to bear and to endure, and not to enjoy.
If you cannot be a great river that bears huge vessels upon its bosom, be a tiny rill that sings down the mountain side, in the lone valley, and gives a drink to the weary traveller.
When you sleep, think that you are resting on the battlefield; when you walk, suspect an ambush in every hedge.
I would not give a half-penny for your love to Jesus if it does not lead you to love everybody else who loves Him.
Hope itself is like a star, not to be seen in the sunshine of prosperity, and only to be discovered in the night of adversity.
Saving faith is an immediate relation to Christ, accepting, receiving, resting upon Him alone, for justification, sanctification, and eternal life.
Repentance is a discovery of the evil of sin, a mourning that we have committed it, a resolution to forsake it.
He who would be little in temptation must be much in prayer.
The temptation of all temptations is to think we can fight temptation in our own strength.
Some Christians try to hide their religion in a corner; let your light shine, says Christ.
Where Jesus comes He comes to reign; in your hearts as in your homes He will brook no rival.
We must learn to take ourselves in hand. We must drag the heart to its duty.
The man whose little sermon is 'repent' sets himself against his age, and will for the time being be battered mercilessly by the age whose moral tone he challenges.
Truly happy is the man whose joy is the Lord, and only the Lord.
If you wish to be miserable, think about yourself; about what you want, what you like, what respect people ought to pay you.
Forget yesterday, except for what you can remember with thankfulness; live today doing your duty cheerfully and thoroughly; leave tomorrow with God.
I look to Jesus and the dove descends; I look to the dove and He disappears.
If we do not love the gospel of Christ, the gospel of Christ will damn us.
There is no doctrine more hated by worldlings, no truth of which they have made such a football, as the great, stupendous, but yet most certain doctrine of the Sovereignty of the infinite Jehovah.
The cross is the centre of two eternities. It looks back on eternity past, and on to eternity to come.
Christ is not valued at all unless He is valued above all.
He who has made his peace with God will, sooner or later, make his peace with men.
I would sooner be holy than happy if the two things could be divorced. Were it possible for a man always to sorrow and yet to be pure, I would choose the sorrow if I might win the purity.
Holiness is not an attainment—it is a Person. Christ in you is your holiness.
A man who is to do much with men must be much with God.
The Word of God is like a lion. You don't have to defend a lion. All you have to do is let the lion loose, and the lion will defend itself.
We must go to Christ for everything. The first thing we should do in the morning, the last thing at night, and the most frequent thing in between, is to apply to Jesus.
He is the wisest man who learns to live above the storm by climbing higher up the mountain of faith.
There are no times in our lives when prayer is more necessary than those times in which we are tempted to think that we can do without it.
If God had not chosen me before I was born, He certainly would not have chosen me afterwards.
Election is sometimes called by ill names by those who do not understand it; but it is the doctrine of those who, knowing themselves, marvel that God should ever have looked upon them in love.
I would not give an inch for your love to Christ if you cannot bear with the brethren.
Of all the things in the world that ought to make a man humble, election is the most humbling.
We may be very poor in this world, but we are very rich in another, if we are children of God.
The fact is, brethren, we want to know more of Christ. Half our doubts and difficulties spring from the slenderness of our acquaintance with Him.
Has God said it? Then it is so. There is no need to ask whether it is reasonable; God has said it, and that is enough.
Whitefield's whole life was an exhibition of holy energy. He never knew what idleness was; nor would he allow himself a moment for it.
Some preachers are like a top — they have to be whipped to make them go.
If we are right with God, He will either spare us from suffering, or He will sustain us in it.
There is more in heaven than there is in your fears.
I would rather have my children sit at the feet of Christ than at the feet of all the doctors of the schools.
Conversion is a deep work — a heart work. It goes throughout the man, throughout the new man, throughout the whole man.
If sinners would be damned, let them leap to hell over our bodies; and if they will perish, let them perish with our arms about their knees.
Christ Jesus came into the world not to make our salvation possible, but to make it certain.
Beware of no man more than yourself; we carry our worst enemies within us.
Pride is the inner garment of the soul; it is the first to be put on and the last to be put off.
If you cannot speak as fluently as others, you can love as deeply.
Faith and works are bound up in the same bundle. He that obeys God trusts God; and he that trusts God obeys God.
If God should give you the choice this morning, I would not have you say, 'Give me a soft path,' but rather, 'Lord, give me grace and a strong heart.'
The Lord, with infinite wisdom, knows just how to balance our joys with our sorrows, our hopes with our fears.
Heaven will be no heaven to me if I do not meet my wife there.
I gather from the apostle's words, that we may pray as much for the dead-while-they-live as we do for those who are physically dead.
Sin is the most expensive thing in the universe — nothing else can cost so much.
It is not so much great talents that God blesses as great likeness to Jesus.
By perseverance the snail reached the ark.
If any man could earn a glance from God by his works, then heaven would be the wages of merit, and not a gift.
Many men owe the grandeur of their lives to their tremendous difficulties.
He who runs from God in the morning will scarcely find Him the rest of the day.
We never know the love of the Father so much as when we kiss His correcting hand.
A true Christian is a man who never gets used to the wonder that God should have loved him.
If Christ has died for me, ungodly as I am, without strength as I am, then I cannot live in sin any longer, but must arouse myself to love and serve Him who has redeemed me.
The Bible is the chart of the voyage of life. He who sails without it is sure to be lost.
Faith is the root, and obedience is the flower; and where there is no flower we may be sure there is no root.
Lord, save us from being unsympathetic Christians who walk in the midst of sinners with no tear in our eyes.
He who waits on God never waits too long.
Brethren, we shall not adjust our Bible to the age; but before we have done with it, by God's grace, we shall adjust the age to the Bible.
An unholy church is useless to the world, and of no esteem among men. It is an abomination, hell's laughter, heaven's abhorrence.
If thou hast the smallest spark of grace, prize it as the apple of thine eye, for it is more precious than gold.
It is good for a man that he should bear the yoke in his youth — the yoke of Christ. There is no other yoke that is easy and no other burden that is light.
